key_insight: Training and inference in deep learning can be reformulated exactly or structurally as Hamilton–Jacobi initial-value problems, linking network weights, PDE dynamics, and convex optimization through a single deformation parameter ε.
engineering_relevance: This provides a unified mathematical lens for designing, analyzing, and potentially hardening architectures such as residual networks, transformers, and recurrent models, with implications for robustness, attribution, and scaling laws.
method: Map gradient-based training to selecting initial data for a viscous Hamilton–Jacobi equation via Hopf–Cole propagation.
Interpret different architectures as discretizations of the same Hamilton–Jacobi class with architecture-specific Hamiltonian and viscosity.
Use the ε-parameter to connect neural networks, tropical algebra, viscous PDEs, and convex optimization in a commutative framework.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports exact correspondence for log-sum-exp layers, structural correspondence for several architecture families, and theoretical results including generalization rate, robustness, and closed-form influence weights.
watch_item: The framework relies on Lipschitz conditions and PDE/discretization assumptions, so practical implementation may be sensitive to model nonlinearity, numerical stability, and architecture-specific approximations.
paper_title: The Hamilton-Jacobi Theory of Deep Learning
